engineGuide.h2: Jeep Cherokee Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jeep Cherokee across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: VM Motori-sourced 2.8 CRD brought modern common-rail diesel to the KJ/KK Cherokee, but water pump design flaws defined replacement demand.
commonFailures: Water pump failure causing overheating and head warping from 70k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear leading to chain stretch and interference damage
Turbocharger actuator sticking resulting in limp mode and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for AR Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: Updated variant of the 2.8 CRD with revised ECU mapping, retaining the same core failure patterns as the AR code.
commonFailures: Water pump seal degradation causing coolant loss and overheating
EGR valve carbon buildup restricting exhaust flow and increasing emissions
Injector seal "Black Death" carbon deposits around injector bases
entries.cta: Get quotes for AVM Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: Final iteration of the 2.8 CRD for the KK facelift, with minor reliability improvements but still susceptible to core cooling system failures.
commonFailures: Water pump impeller wear causing reduced coolant circulation
Timing chain guide plastic fatigue leading to rattle and potential failure
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey vehicles causing soot accumulation
entries.cta: Get quotes for ECN Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output variant of the 2.2 Multijet for Trailhawk and performance trims, sharing core failure patterns with the base code.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear causing starting difficulties and power loss
Turbocharger wastegate actuator failure resulting in over/under-boost conditions
Injector carbon buildup leading to rough idle and misfire codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for 68140606AB Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Late-production 2.2 Multijet code with minor revisions to injector and turbo components, but core architecture remains unchanged.
commonFailures: AdBlue injector clogging causing NOx sensor faults and limp mode
Turbocharger oil feed line coking leading to bearing failure
Injector seal degradation causing diesel leakage into cylinder head
entries.cta: Get quotes for 04792572AB Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Classic VM Motori 2.5 TD powered early XJ Cherokees; robust but prone to head gasket and injector issues at high mileage.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure causing coolant/oil mixing and overheating
Injector pump wear leading to hard starting and black smoke
Rocker arm shaft wear resulting in valvetrain noise and reduced performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for ENC Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Updated casting of the 4.0 PowerTech with minor revisions; shares core reliability profile with earlier ERH-coded units.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure from overheating causing coolant loss and white smoke
Crankshaft position sensor failure resulting in intermittent stalling and no-start
Rocker arm wear leading to valvetrain noise and reduced power output
Intake manifold gasket leaks causing rough idle and vacuum codes
Oil pressure sender failure triggering false warning lights
Thermostat housing cracks leading to coolant loss and overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for 53010442 Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 3.7L Petrol
entries.history: Compact 3.7 V6 offered smoother operation than the 4.0 but with lower torque; timing chain and oil consumption issues noted.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattle and potential interference damage
Oil consumption from valve stem seal wear on high-mileage units
Intake manifold runner control failure triggering check engine lights
entries.cta: Get quotes for EKG Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Modern Pentastar 3.2 V6 brought improved efficiency and power to the KL Cherokee; generally reliable but cylinder head issues reported.
commonFailures: Cylinder head crack on early production units causing coolant loss
Variable valve timing solenoid clogging leading to rough idle and power loss
Oil cooler gasket leaks resulting in oil/coolant mixing
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERB Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: Fiat-sourced 2.4 Tigershark MultiAir offered efficiency but oil consumption and MultiAir actuator issues drive replacement demand.
commonFailures: MultiAir actuator failure causing loss of valve control and misfires
Excessive oil consumption from piston ring wear on early units
Timing chain tensioner wear leading to rattle and potential failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4884029AA Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Advanced 2.0 Hurricane turbo with eTorque mild-hybrid system; complex but efficient, with turbo and hybrid component concerns.
commonFailures: Turbocharger wastegate actuator failure causing boost control issues
eTorque belt-driven starter-generator wear leading to hybrid system faults
High-pressure fuel pump wear resulting in hard starting and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4884030AC Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Cherokee Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Cherokee's diesel and petrol engines have distinct reliability profiles: the 2.8 CRD suffers water pump and timing chain issues, while the 2.2 Multijet faces injector seal "Black Death" and turbo failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Jeep Cherokee 2.8 CRD Water Pump Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Cherokee 2.8 CRD Water Pump Failure - Why Cooling System Collapse Destroys Engines
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Cherokee KJ/KK 2.8 CRD (2002-2013, AR/AVM/ECN engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Plastic impeller water pump design degrades, causing coolant loss, overheating, and cylinder head warping on interference diesel engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat, coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before overheating; head must be undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine surface, replace gasket, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block is undamaged; risk of hidden cracks remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded water pump, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage vehicles; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: If your 2.8 CRD has overheated, a rebuilt engine is often more cost-effective than head repair, especially on Cherokees worth under £5,000. We recommend comparing quotes before committing to partial repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.8 CRD engine prices for your Jeep Cherokee

problems.group: Jeep Cherokee Black Death Injector Seals
problems.h4: Jeep Cherokee Black Death Injector Seals - Why Diesel Carbon Buildup Kills 2.2 Multijet Engines
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Cherokee KL 2.2 Multijet (2014-2022, 55282643/68140606AB engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Injector copper seal failure allows diesel to leak into cylinder head, creating carbon deposits that seize injectors and damage the head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, clean carbon, replace copper seals and washers
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if injectors are not seized; early intervention critical

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace seized injectors, machine head surface, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if head damage is superficial; risk of recurrence remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded injector seals, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution for high-mileage KL Cherokees

problems.recommendation: Black Death is progressive - if you notice diesel smell or hard starting, act immediately. For Cherokees over 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded seals offers better value than repeated injector repairs.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned 2.2 Multijet engine with upgraded seals for your Jeep Cherokee

problems.h4: Jeep Cherokee 2.2 Multijet Turbo Failure - Why Boost Loss Means Engine Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Cherokee KL 2.2 Multijet (2014-2022, all variants)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger vane sticking from carbon buildup causes over-boost or under-boost conditions, leading to limp mode and potential engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean turbo vanes, replace actuator, reset ECU adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if turbo internals are undamaged; early symptom stage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace turbocharger assembly, replace oil feed lines
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine has not suffered oil starvation; risk of recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, new oil lines, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if engine has suffered oil starvation or bearing damage

problems.recommendation: If your Cherokee shows whistling noises or power loss, get a turbo health check immediately. For vehicles over 90k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components often provides better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 2.2 Multijet rebuilt engines with upgraded turbos for your Jeep Cherokee

problems.h4: Jeep Cherokee Timing Chain Failure - Why Interference Engines Demand Immediate Action
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Cherokee KJ/KK 2.8 CRD, KL 2.2 Multijet (all diesel variants)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain stretch; on interference diesel engines, chain skip causes piston-valve contact and catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioners, guides before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable as preventative maintenance; not possible after damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after chain skip; prevents recurrence with upgraded parts

problems.recommendation: Timing chain failure is catastrophic on Cherokee diesel engines. If you hear rattle or have a no-start, replacement is the only viable option. Compare rebuilt engine quotes before authorising repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt diesel engine prices for your Jeep Cherokee before chain failure

problems.group: Jeep Cherokee 4.0 PowerTech Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Cherokee 4.0 PowerTech Head Gasket Failure - Why Overheating Demands Engine Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Cherokee XJ/KJ 4.0 PowerTech (1984-2007, ERH/53010442 engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warping from overheating breaches the head gasket, allowing coolant/oil mixing and compression loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine surface, replace gasket, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block is undamaged; risk of hidden cracks on high-mileage units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded head gasket, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for classic XJ Cherokees; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: For XJ Cherokees worth £3,000-£6,000, a rebuilt 4.0 engine often costs less than head repair plus ancillary work. Compare quotes before committing to partial repairs.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned 4.0 PowerTech engine for your classic Jeep Cherokee

problems.h4: Jeep Cherokee Oil Leak & Seizure - Why Ignoring Leaks Leads to Total Engine Failure
problems.affectedModels: All Jeep Cherokee variants, all engine codes
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Variable; seizure often occurs after prolonged oil loss
problems.rootCause: Rear main seal, oil pan, or valve cover gasket leaks cause low oil pressure; continued driving leads to bearing failure and engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace leaking seal/gasket, refill oil, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if engine has not suffered oil starvation damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with all new seals, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after seizure; prevents recurrence with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: If your Cherokee shows oil pressure warnings or unusual noises, stop driving immediately. For seized engines, a rebuilt unit is the only viable solution - compare quotes before authorising work.
problems.cta: Get urgent quotes for seized Jeep Cherokee engine replacement from UK specialists

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Cherokee
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing water pump failure on a 2.8 CRD, Black Death on a 2.2 Multijet, or head gasket issues on a classic 4.0, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Jeep Cherokee specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Cherokee Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Cherokee and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Jeep Cherokee Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Jeep Cherokee across UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Jeep Cherokee 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L Multijet diesel powered the modern KL Cherokee (2014-2022), offering improved efficiency over the older 2.8 CRD but introducing injector seal and turbo reliability concerns that drive replacement demand.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Cherokee 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Cherokee 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The classic 2.5L VM Motori diesel powered early XJ Cherokees (1984-2001); robust but prone to head gasket and injector pump wear at high mileage, making it a common replacement candidate for classic restorations.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Cherokee 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Cherokee 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8L VM Motori CRD was the dominant diesel for KJ/KK Cherokees (2002-2013), but water pump design flaws and timing chain tensioner wear make it the most frequently replaced engine in the Cherokee range.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Cherokee 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Cherokee 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The advanced 2.0L Hurricane turbo with eTorque mild-hybrid system powered late KL Cherokees (2019-2022); complex but efficient, with turbo and hybrid component concerns driving specialist replacement enquiries.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Cherokee 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Cherokee 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4L Tigershark MultiAir petrol offered efficiency for KL Cherokees (2014-2019) but suffered from MultiAir actuator failures and oil consumption issues that frequently lead to replacement enquiries.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Cherokee 2.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Cherokee 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The modern 3.2L Pentastar V6 brought strong performance to KL Cherokees (2014-2022); generally reliable but early-production cylinder head cracks and VVT solenoid issues drive replacement demand.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Cherokee 3.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Cherokee 3.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The compact 3.7L PowerTech V6 offered smoother operation than the 4.0 for KJ/KK Cherokees (2002-2013), but timing chain tensioner wear and oil consumption issues make it a common replacement candidate.
commonFailurePoints: oil consumption from valve stem seals
items.cta: Compare Jeep Cherokee 3.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Cherokee 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary AMC-derived 4.0L straight-six defined the XJ Cherokee (1984-2001) and early KJ models; robust but head gasket failures and sensor issues drive replacement enquiries for classic restorations.
items.cta: Compare Jeep Cherokee 4.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Jeep Cherokee Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Jeep Cherokee engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 2.5 TD classics to the latest 2.2 Multijet and 2.0 Hurricane units.

items.title: Jeep Cherokee Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines dominate Cherokee replacement enquiries, with the 2.8 CRD (KJ/KK) and 2.2 Multijet (KL) accounting for the majority of high-intent searches due to known cooling and injector failure patterns.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominate Cherokee replacement enquiries, with the 2.8 CRD (KJ/KK) and 2.2 Multijet (KL) accounting for the majority of high-intent searches due to known cooling and injector failure patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Jeep Cherokee Diesel Engine Families
families: AR/AVM/ECN (2.8 CRD) - KJ/KK workhorse, water pump and timing chain concerns
55282643/68140606AB (2.2 Multijet) - KL efficiency leader, injector seal "Black Death" risk
ENC (2.5 TD) - Classic XJ robust unit, head gasket and injector pump wear
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for towing and off-road use
Water pump failures on 2.8 CRD causing catastrophic overheating
Injector seal "Black Death" carbon buildup on 2.2 Multijet units
High UK replacement demand on high-mileage workhorse Cherokees

items.title: Jeep Cherokee Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Cherokees appeal to enthusiasts and classic restorers, with the iconic 4.0 straight-six and modern 3.2 Pentastar V6 driving most replacement enquiries despite generally better reliability than diesel variants.
items.descriptor: Petrol Cherokees appeal to enthusiasts and classic restorers, with the iconic 4.0 straight-six and modern 3.2 Pentastar V6 driving most replacement enquiries despite generally better reliability than diesel variants.
items.familiesLabel: Common Jeep Cherokee Petrol Engine Families
families: ERH/53010442 (4.0 PowerTech) - Legendary XJ straight-six, head gasket concerns
EKG (3.7 V6) - Smooth KJ/KK unit, timing chain tensioner wear
ERB (3.2 Pentastar) - Modern KL performance, early head crack issues
4884029AA (2.4 Tigershark) - Efficient KL unit, MultiAir actuator failures
4884030AC (2.0 Hurricane) - Advanced MHEV KL unit, turbo/hybrid complexity
knownFor: The 4.0L straight-six is revered for simplicity and tunability
Head gasket failures on high-mileage 4.0L units after overheating
MultiAir actuator failures on 2.4L Tigershark causing misfires
Cylinder head cracks on early 3.2L Pentastar production units

items.title: Jeep Cherokee Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: The 2.0L Hurricane with eTorque mild-hybrid system represents the most advanced Cherokee powertrain, offering strong performance with improved efficiency but increased complexity for replacement considerations.
items.descriptor: The 2.0L Hurricane with eTorque mild-hybrid system represents the most advanced Cherokee powertrain, offering strong performance with improved efficiency but increased complexity for replacement considerations.
families: 4884030AC (2.0L Hurricane) - KL LCI performance variant with eTorque
48-volt belt-driven starter-generator provides torque assist and regenerative braking
Petrol engine handles primary propulsion; electric motor assists under acceleration
Hybrid system requires specialist diagnostics during engine replacement
importantNotes: MHEV engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection and recalibration
Availability of rebuilt MHEV units is more limited than standard engines
Specialist fitting recommended due to integrated electrical architecture

engineYears.h2: Jeep Cherokee Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Jeep Cherokee's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The original XJ Cherokee established the model's reputation, powered by robust AMC-derived petrol engines and VM Motori diesels that remain popular with enthusiasts today.
keyChanges: Introduction of the legendary AMC 4.0L straight-six petrol engine
VM Motori 2.5L turbo diesel added for European markets
Facelift updates in 1997 with revised interior and minor mechanical updates
knownFor: The 4.0L straight-six is revered for simplicity, torque and tunability
Head gasket failures on high-mileage 4.0L units after overheating
2.5 TD robust but prone to injector pump wear at high mileage
Strong enthusiast community supports classic XJ restoration projects
years.cta: Get quotes for 1984-2001 Jeep Cherokee replacement engines
years.ticker: XJ 4.0 head gasket repair • 2.5 TD injector pump replacement • Cherokee classic engine rebuild cost

years.description: The boxy KJ Cherokee introduced modern common-rail diesel and updated petrol options, but water pump design flaws on the 2.8 CRD defined replacement demand for this generation.
keyChanges: New unibody platform replacing the XJ's body-on-frame design
Introduction of VM Motori 2.8L CRD diesel alongside carryover 4.0L petrol
Chrysler 3.7L PowerTech V6 added as mid-range petrol option
knownFor: 2.8 CRD water pump failures causing catastrophic overheating from 70k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear on 2.8 CRD leading to interference damage
4.0L remains reliable but head gasket issues persist on high-mileage units
KJ Cherokees often used as high-mileage workhorses, driving replacement demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2007 Jeep Cherokee replacement engines
years.ticker: KJ 2.8 CRD water pump failure • Cherokee 4.0 head gasket cost • 3.7 V6 timing chain replacement

years.description: The KK facelift refined the KJ platform with updated styling and minor mechanical revisions, but the 2.8 CRD's core failure patterns remained unchanged, sustaining high replacement enquiry volumes.
keyChanges: Exterior and interior facelift with revised lighting and dashboard
ECN variant of 2.8 CRD introduced with minor ECU mapping updates
3.7L V6 petrol continued as the sole petrol option
knownFor: Water pump and timing chain issues persist on updated 2.8 CRD
DPF regeneration problems on short-journey diesel Cherokees
3.7L V6 oil consumption from valve stem seal wear at high mileage
KK Cherokees remain popular as affordable 4x4s, driving budget replacement searches
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2013 Jeep Cherokee replacement engines
years.ticker: KK 2.8 CRD turbo failure • Cherokee DPF blockage cost • 3.7 V6 oil consumption fix

years.preview: KL Generation Launch: New Platform, New Engines
years.description: The all-new KL Cherokee introduced Fiat-derived 2.2 Multijet diesel and Tigershark petrol engines, bringing modern efficiency but new failure patterns that now dominate replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: All-new platform with independent rear suspension and advanced electronics
Fiat-sourced 2.2L Multijet II diesel replaced the VM Motori 2.8 CRD
2.4L Tigershark MultiAir and 3.2L Pentastar V6 petrol options introduced
knownFor: 2.2 Multijet "Black Death" injector seal failures driving high replacement demand
MultiAir actuator failures on 2.4L Tigershark causing misfires and power loss
Early 3.2L Pentastar cylinder head cracks on pre-2016 production units
KL Cherokees command higher replacement budgets due to newer age and complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2018 Jeep Cherokee replacement engines
years.ticker: KL 2.2 Multijet Black Death • Cherokee 2.4 MultiAir actuator • 3.2 Pentastar head crack repair

years.description: The late-production KL facelift introduced the advanced 2.0L Hurricane turbo with eTorque mild-hybrid system, representing the most complex Cherokee powertrain to date.
keyChanges: Introduction of 2.0L Hurricane turbo with 48V eTorque mild-hybrid system
Updated infotainment and driver assistance systems
Minor revisions to 2.2 Multijet and 3.2 Pentastar for improved reliability
knownFor: 2.0 Hurricane offers strong performance but turbo and hybrid complexity increase repair costs
Late-production 2.2 Multijet units show improved injector seal reliability
3.2L Pentastar head crack issues largely resolved on post-2016 units
KL LCI Cherokees attract enthusiast owners willing to invest in quality rebuilt engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2022 Jeep Cherokee replacement engines
years.ticker: KL 2.0 Hurricane turbo cost • Cherokee eTorque system repair • 2.2 Multijet late-model reliability

faq.h2: Jeep Cherokee Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Jeep Cherokee engine in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices for a Jeep Cherokee range from £1,200 for a used 2.5 TD unit to £5,200 for a rebuilt 2.0 Hurricane MHEV, with supply & fit adding £400-£900 depending on location and complexity.
keyPoints: 2.8 CRD rebuilt engines: £1,800-£4,200 supply only
2.2 Multijet rebuilt engines: £2,200-£4,800 supply only
4.0 PowerTech rebuilt engines: £1,500-£3,900 supply only
Supply & fit service adds £400-£900 to engine price
All quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Jeep Cherokee engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Jeep Cherokee?
items.answer: For Cherokees valued under £5,000, a rebuilt engine often costs more than the vehicle's worth, but for well-maintained examples or enthusiast restorations, replacement can extend life cost-effectively versus buying another used 4x4.
keyPoints: Compare rebuilt engine cost against current Cherokee market value
Factor in recent maintenance history and overall vehicle condition
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components reduce recurrence risk
Consider supply & fit convenience versus DIY installation savings
Warranty coverage provides peace of mind for high-mileage investments
items.cta: Get a tailored quote for your high-mileage Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

items.question: What causes the Jeep Cherokee "Black Death" injector seal failure?
items.answer: Black Death occurs when copper injector seals fail on 2.2 Multijet diesel engines, allowing diesel to leak into the cylinder head and create hard carbon deposits that seize injectors and damage the head.
keyPoints: Affects KL Cherokee 2.2 Multijet engines (2014-2022)
Symptoms include diesel smell, hard starting, and carbon around injectors
Early intervention with seal replacement can prevent catastrophic damage
Rebuilt engines with upgraded seals eliminate recurrence risk
Regular servicing and quality fuel help delay seal degradation
items.cta: Find a 2.2 Multijet rebuilt engine with upgraded injector seals

items.question: Which Jeep Cherokee engine is the most reliable for replacement?
items.answer: The classic 4.0L PowerTech straight-six petrol engine has the strongest reliability reputation among Cherokee engines, with simple design and widespread parts availability making rebuilt units a dependable choice for enthusiasts.
keyPoints: 4.0L PowerTech: simple design, robust internals, easy to rebuild
2.5 TD VM Motori: robust but injector pump wear at high mileage
2.8 CRD: prone to water pump and timing chain failures
2.2 Multijet: efficient but injector seal and turbo concerns
Always choose rebuilt engines with upgraded known weak points
items.cta: Compare 4.0 PowerTech rebuilt engine prices for your classic Cherokee

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code into my Jeep Cherokee?
items.answer: Engine swaps between Cherokee generations are complex due to ECU, wiring harness and mounting differences; we recommend matching the exact engine code to your original variant for guaranteed compatibility and warranty validity.
keyPoints: ECU and wiring harness must match engine code for proper operation
Mounting points and ancillary layouts vary between generations
Using incorrect engine codes may void warranty and cause running issues
Enter your registration to verify the exact compatible engine codes
Our specialists can advise on rare cross-generation compatibility cases
items.cta: Verify compatible engine codes for your Jeep Cherokee variant

items.question: How long does a Jeep Cherokee engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 2-5 working days from stock; supply & fit installations require 1-3 days at a specialist workshop depending on engine accessibility and ancillary component replacement.
keyPoints: Stock rebuilt engines dispatch within 24-48 hours
Non-stock codes may require 5-10 day rebuild lead time
Supply & fit installations: 1 day for accessible engines, 2-3 days for complex variants
Post-installation commissioning and road test included
Nationwide delivery available to your chosen specialist workshop
items.cta: Get urgent quotes for fast Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Jeep Cherokee engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects, with some specialists offering extended coverage options.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Coverage includes parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Warranty valid at any UK workshop, not just original fitter
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Extended warranty options available from select specialists
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Jeep Cherokee rebuilt engines

items.question: Do you supply engines for classic Jeep Cherokee XJ models?
items.answer: Yes, we supply rebuilt and used engines for classic XJ Cherokees (1984-2001), including the iconic 4.0L straight-six and 2.5L VM Motori diesel, with specialists experienced in classic 4x4 restorations.
keyPoints: 4.0L PowerTech rebuilt engines: £1,500-£3,900 supply only
2.5 TD VM Motori rebuilt engines: £1,200-£2,600 supply only
Specialists familiar with classic Cherokee mounting and ancillaries
Used low-mileage donor engines available for budget restorations
Enter your XJ registration to verify exact compatible engine codes
items.cta: Find a rebuilt engine for your classic Jeep Cherokee XJ

items.question: What should I do if my Jeep Cherokee engine has seized?
items.answer: If your Cherokee engine has seized, stop driving immediately to prevent further damage; a rebuilt engine is typically the only viable solution, and comparing quotes before authorising work ensures best value.
keyPoints: Seizure usually indicates bearing failure from oil starvation or overheating
Partial repairs rarely viable after catastrophic seizure
Rebuilt engines include new bearings and upgraded components
Supply & fit service handles removal of seized unit safely
Act quickly to minimise secondary damage to transmission or drivetrain
items.cta: Get urgent quotes for seized Jeep Cherokee engine replacement

items.question: How do I find my Jeep Cherokee engine code?
items.answer: Your Cherokee engine code is stamped on the engine block, listed on the V5C logbook under "engine number", or instantly verified by entering your registration on our platform for precise compatibility matching.
keyPoints: Check V5C logbook for engine number reference
Engine code stamped on block near cylinder head or timing cover
Common codes: AR/AVM/ECN (2.8 CRD), 55282643 (2.2 Multijet), ERH (4.0)
Enter registration for instant DVLA-verified engine identification
Matching exact code prevents compatibility issues and warranty voidance
items.cta: Identify your Jeep Cherokee engine code and get compatible quotes
